RULING:
PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as amended: Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted per C.C.P. 36(a). Trial is September 3, 2013 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day for hearing summary motions is August 27, 2013. Summary motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant sections of the C.C.P. The time to respond to written discovery is shortened to 20 days. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendant(s) no later than August 9, 2013. Responses to the demand are due no later than August 16, 2013. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on August 27, 2013. Fact discovery cut-off date is August 16, 2013. Expert discovery cut-off date is August 30, 2013. No later than May 28, 2013, plaintiff must provide a precise statement of facts stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than May 28, 2013, defendants must provide a precise statement of facts stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. The parties to meet and confer regarding any other scheduling or other issue which any of them want to have included in the preference order. Further case management conference on July 10, 2013 at 1:30pm. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on September 3, 2013 at 1:30pm in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by August 29, 2013 at 2:00pm. Counsel for plaintiff to prepare an order. Judge: Teri L.
